result.finding: HTTP/REST is the sole communication protocol. The application exposes HTTP endpoints via Spring MVC (@GetMapping, @PostMapping) for both server-rendered HTML (Thymeleaf) and a JSON REST endpoint. No gRPC, message queue, WebSocket, or TCP communication found.
evidence: @GetMapping and @PostMapping annotations found in OwnerController, PetController, VisitController, VetController, WelcomeController, CrashController
VetController uses @ResponseBody to serve JSON at /vets endpoint
No RabbitMQ, Kafka, or JMS dependencies in pom.xml
No WebSocket or SignalR usage found
spring-boot-starter-web provides embedded Tomcat HTTP server
values: Endpoints: GET /, GET /owners, POST /owners/new, GET /owners/find, GET/POST /owners/{id}/edit, GET/POST /owners/{id}/pets/new, GET/POST /owners/{id}/pets/{id}/edit, GET/POST /owners/{id}/pets/{id}/visits/new, GET /vets.html, GET /vets (JSON), GET /oups
HTML views: all other endpoints return Thymeleaf-rendered HTML
